# Semantic conventions for Network Flows

This document defines semantic conventions for Network flows.

<!-- toc -->

- [Metrics](#metrics)
  - [Metric `network.flow.bytes`](#metric-networkflowbytes)
  - [Metric `network.inter.zone.bytes`](#metric-networkinterzonebytes)

<!-- tocstop -->

## Metrics

### Metric `network.flow.bytes`

<!-- semconv metric.network.flow.bytes -->
<!-- NOTE: THIS TEXT IS AUTOGENERATED. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->
<!-- see templates/registry/markdown/snippet.md.j2 -->
<!-- prettier-ignore-start -->

| Name | Instrument Type | Unit (UCUM) | Description | Stability | Entity Associations |
| -------- | --------------- | ----------- | -------------- | --------- | ------ |
| `network.flow.bytes` | Counter | `By` | Bytes transferred from a source endpoint to a destination endpoint. [1] | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | |

**[1]:** This metric describes point-to-point network traffic: the number of bytes being transmitted from the
network source (or grouped sources) to a network destination (or group of destinations).

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | [Requirement Level](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/general/attribute-requirement-level/) | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`destination.cidr`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [1] | string | The CIDR range that matched the destination address. [2] | `192.168.0.0/16` |
| [`destination.cloud.availability_zone`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [3] | string | The cloud availability zone associated with the destination side of the flow. | `us-east-1d` |
| [`destination.k8s.namespace.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` when the flow is observed in Kubernetes. | string | The name of the Kubernetes namespace associated with the destination side of the flow. | `backend` |
| [`destination.k8s.node.ip`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [4] | string | The IP address of the Kubernetes node associated with the destination side of the flow. | `10.0.34.56` |
| [`destination.k8s.node.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [5] | string | The name of the Kubernetes node associated with the destination side of the flow. | `ip-10-0-34-56.ec2.internal` |
| [`destination.k8s.owner.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [6] | string | The name of the Kubernetes workload owner associated with the destination side of the flow. | `backend` |
| [`destination.k8s.owner.type`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [7] | string | The Kubernetes workload owner type associated with the destination side of the flow. | `Deployment`; `DaemonSet` |
| [`destination.k8s.pod.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [8] | string | The name of the Kubernetes Pod associated with the destination side of the flow. | `backend-76d8f7c6d9-4n1qp` |
| [`destination.k8s.resource.type`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [9] | string | The Kubernetes resource type associated with the destination side of the flow. | `Pod`; `Service` |
| [`destination.port`](/docs/registry/attributes/destination.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [10] | int | Destination port number | `3389`; `2888` |
| [`k8s.cluster.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/k8s.md) | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | `Recommended` when the flow is observed in Kubernetes. | string | The name of the cluster. | `opentelemetry-cluster` |
| [`network.local.address`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | `Recommended` | string | Local address of the network connection - IP address or Unix domain socket name. [11] | `10.1.2.80`; `/tmp/my.sock` |
| [`source.cidr`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [12] | string | The CIDR range that matched the source address. [13] | `10.0.0.0/8` |
| [`source.cloud.availability_zone`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [14] | string | The cloud availability zone associated with the source side of the flow. | `us-east-1c` |
| [`source.k8s.namespace.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` when the flow is observed in Kubernetes. | string | The name of the Kubernetes namespace associated with the source side of the flow. | `frontend` |
| [`source.k8s.node.ip`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [15] | string | The IP address of the Kubernetes node associated with the source side of the flow. | `10.0.12.34` |
| [`source.k8s.node.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [16] | string | The name of the Kubernetes node associated with the source side of the flow. | `ip-10-0-12-34.ec2.internal` |
| [`source.k8s.owner.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [17] | string | The name of the Kubernetes workload owner associated with the source side of the flow. | `frontend` |
| [`source.k8s.owner.type`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [18] | string | The Kubernetes workload owner type associated with the source side of the flow. | `Deployment`; `Daemonset` |
| [`source.k8s.pod.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [19] | string | The name of the Kubernetes Pod associated with the source side of the flow. | `frontend-7c9d4b6d7f-9kz2m` |
| [`source.k8s.resource.type`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [20] | string | The Kubernetes resource type associated with the source side of the flow. | `Node`; `Pod` |
| [`destination.address`](/docs/registry/attributes/destination.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | Destination address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name. [21] | `destination.example.com`; `10.1.2.80`; `/tmp/my.sock` |
| [`destination.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | Anything that identifies the destination of the network flow, like a network host name a container name, or an IP address. Human-friendly names are preferred over numeric addresses | `subdomain.example.org`; `192.33.22.12` |
| [`network.interface.direction`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | The direction of the network flow as seen from the network interface. [22] | `ingress`; `egress` |
| [`network.interface.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | The network interface name. [23] | `lo`; `eth0` |
| [`network.protocol.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | `Opt-In` | string | [OSI application layer](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Application_layer) or non-OSI equivalent. [24] | `amqp`; `http`; `mqtt` |
| [`network.transport`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | `Opt-In` | string | [OSI transport layer](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Transport_layer) or [inter-process communication method](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Inter-process_communication). [25] | `tcp`; `udp` |
| [`network.type`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | `Opt-In` | string | [OSI network layer](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Network_layer) or non-OSI equivalent. [26] | `ipv4`; `ipv6` |
| [`source.address`](/docs/registry/attributes/source.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | Source address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name. [27] | `source.example.com`; `10.1.2.80`; `/tmp/my.sock` |
| [`source.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | Anything that identifies the source of the network flow, like a network host name, a container name, or an IP address. Human-friendly names are preferred over numeric addresses | `subdomain.example.org`; `192.33.22.12` |
| [`source.port`](/docs/registry/attributes/source.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | int | Source port number | `3389`; `2888` |

**[1] `destination.cidr`:** when the destination address is classified into configured CIDR ranges and there is no other low-cardinality attribute to group the network flow destination.

**[2] `destination.cidr`:** The instrumentation can associate addresses with CIDR ranges.
This value can be derived from the host network configuration,
or can match a user-defined value that is provided to the
instrumentation.

**[3] `destination.cloud.availability_zone`:** when destination-side cloud zone information is available.

**[4] `destination.k8s.node.ip`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[5] `destination.k8s.node.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[6] `destination.k8s.owner.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[7] `destination.k8s.owner.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[8] `destination.k8s.pod.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need pod granularity for metrics.
It might cause high metrics cardinality.

**[9] `destination.k8s.resource.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need resource granularity for metrics.

**[10] `destination.port`:** when the same host runs multiple service instances, for differentiation

**[11] `network.local.address`:** This value is the IP address of the node where the network observability agent runs. It allows
deduplicating information of the same flow when source and destination are in different nodes
and thus they are observed by agents both in the source and destination nodes.

**[12] `source.cidr`:** when the source address is classified into configured CIDR ranges and there is no other low-cardinality attribute to group the network flow source.

**[13] `source.cidr`:** The instrumentation can associate addresses with CIDR ranges.
This value can be derived from the host network configuration,
or can match a user-defined value that is provided to the
instrumentation.

**[14] `source.cloud.availability_zone`:** when source-side cloud zone information is available.

**[15] `source.k8s.node.ip`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[16] `source.k8s.node.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[17] `source.k8s.owner.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[18] `source.k8s.owner.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[19] `source.k8s.pod.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need pod granularity for metrics.
It might cause high metrics cardinality.

**[20] `source.k8s.resource.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need resource granularity for metrics.

**[21] `destination.address`:** When observed from the source side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `destination.address` SHOULD represent the destination address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

**[22] `network.interface.direction`:** This field does not necessarily match with incoming/outgoing traffic as
seen from the host or application level.

For example, imagine a container sending network data
through a virtual interface veth0 that is connected to the host's physical
interface eth1. If the network probes are attached to veth0, the
traffic would be observed as "egress". However, if the network probes are
attached to eth1, the traffic would be observed as "ingress", because
eth1 is receiving the data from the connected veth0 interface.

**[23] `network.interface.name`:** Provides granularity about the different network interfaces where the
network traffic is being observed. For example, a host with two network
interfaces eth0 and eth1 could send/receive traffic through both interfaces,
but it can also contain names for the internal virtual interfaces that would
enable the communication between containers in the same host.

**[24] `network.protocol.name`:** The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

**[25] `network.transport`:** The transport protocol of the observed network traffic.

**[26] `network.type`:** The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

**[27] `source.address`:** When observed from the destination side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `source.address` SHOULD represent the source address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

---

`network.interface.direction` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `egress` | The flow was observed as egress traffic. | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `ingress` | The flow was observed as ingress traffic. | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

---

`network.transport` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `pipe` | Named or anonymous pipe. | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `quic` | QUIC | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `tcp` | TCP | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `udp` | UDP | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `unix` | Unix domain socket | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |

---

`network.type` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ipv4` | IPv4 | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `ipv6` | IPv6 | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |

<!-- prettier-ignore-end -->
<!-- END AUTOGENERATED TEXT -->
<!-- endsemconv -->

### Metric `network.inter.zone.bytes`

<!-- semconv metric.network.inter.zone.bytes -->
<!-- NOTE: THIS TEXT IS AUTOGENERATED. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->
<!-- see templates/registry/markdown/snippet.md.j2 -->
<!-- prettier-ignore-start -->

| Name | Instrument Type | Unit (UCUM) | Description | Stability | Entity Associations |
| -------- | --------------- | ----------- | -------------- | --------- | ------ |
| `network.inter.zone.bytes` | Counter | `By` | Bytes transferred between source and destination cloud availability zones. [1] | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | |

**[1]:** This metric is intended for reduced-cardinality reporting of inter-zone traffic.
It can be emitted alongside `network.flow.bytes` without requiring zone attributes on every flow datapoint.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | [Requirement Level](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/general/attribute-requirement-level/) | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| [`destination.cloud.availability_zone`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Recommended` [3] | string | The cloud availability zone associated with the destination side of the flow. | `us-east-1d` |

Human-friendly names are preferred over numeric addresses | `subdomain.example.org`; `192.33.22.12` |
| [`network.interface.direction`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | The direction of the network flow as seen from the network interface. [22] | `ingress`; `egress` |

[25] | `tcp`; `udp` |
| [`network.type`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | `Opt-In` | string | [OSI network layer](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Network_layer) or non-OSI equivalent. [26] | `ipv4`; `ipv6` |
| [`source.address`](/docs/registry/attributes/source.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | Source address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name. [27] | `source.example.com`; `10.1.2.80`; `/tmp/my.sock` |
| [`source.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/network.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | string | Anything that identifies the source of the network flow, like a network host name, a container name, or an IP address. Human-friendly names are preferred over numeric addresses | `subdomain.example.org`; `192.33.22.12` |
| [`source.port`](/docs/registry/attributes/source.md) | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | `Opt-In` | int | Source port number | `3389`; `2888` |

**[1] `destination.cidr`:** when the destination address is classified into configured CIDR ranges and there is no other low-cardinality attribute to group the network flow destination.

**[2] `destination.cidr`:** The instrumentation can associate addresses with CIDR ranges.
This value can be derived from the host network configuration,
or can match a user-defined value that is provided to the
instrumentation.

**[3] `destination.cloud.availability_zone`:** when destination-side cloud zone information is available.

**[4] `destination.k8s.node.ip`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[5] `destination.k8s.node.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[6] `destination.k8s.owner.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[7] `destination.k8s.owner.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[8] `destination.k8s.pod.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need pod granularity for metrics.
It might cause high metrics cardinality.

**[9] `destination.k8s.resource.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need resource granularity for metrics.

**[10] `destination.port`:** when the same host runs multiple service instances, for differentiation

**[11] `network.local.address`:** This value is the IP address of the node where the network observability agent runs. It allows
deduplicating information of the same flow when source and destination are in different nodes
and thus they are observed by agents both in the source and destination nodes.

**[12] `source.cidr`:** when the source address is classified into configured CIDR ranges and there is no other low-cardinality attribute to group the network flow source.

**[13] `source.cidr`:** The instrumentation can associate addresses with CIDR ranges.
This value can be derived from the host network configuration,
or can match a user-defined value that is provided to the
instrumentation.

**[14] `source.cloud.availability_zone`:** when source-side cloud zone information is available.

**[15] `source.k8s.node.ip`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[16] `source.k8s.node.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need node granularity for metrics.

**[17] `source.k8s.owner.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[18] `source.k8s.owner.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need owner granularity for metrics.

**[19] `source.k8s.pod.name`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need pod granularity for metrics.
It might cause high metrics cardinality.

**[20] `source.k8s.resource.type`:** when the flow is observed in Kubernetes and you need resource granularity for metrics.

**[21] `destination.address`:** When observed from the source side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `destination.address` SHOULD represent the destination address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

**[22] `network.interface.direction`:** This field does not necessarily match with incoming/outgoing traffic as
seen from the host or application level.

For example, imagine a container sending network data
through a virtual interface veth0 that is connected to the host's physical
interface eth1. If the network probes are attached to veth0, the
traffic would be observed as "egress". However, if the network probes are
attached to eth1, the traffic would be observed as "ingress", because
eth1 is receiving the data from the connected veth0 interface.

**[23] `network.interface.name`:** Provides granularity about the different network interfaces where the
network traffic is being observed. For example, a host with two network
interfaces eth0 and eth1 could send/receive traffic through both interfaces,
but it can also contain names for the internal virtual interfaces that would
enable the communication between containers in the same host.

**[24] `network.protocol.name`:** The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

**[25] `network.transport`:** The transport protocol of the observed network traffic.

**[26] `network.type`:** The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

**[27] `source.address`:** When observed from the destination side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `source.address` SHOULD represent the source address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

---

`network.interface.direction` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `egress` | The flow was observed as egress traffic. | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `ingress` | The flow was observed as ingress traffic. | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

---

`network.transport` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `pipe` | Named or anonymous pipe. | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `quic` | QUIC | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `tcp` | TCP | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `udp` | UDP | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `unix` | Unix domain socket | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |

---

`network.type` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ipv4` | IPv4 | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `ipv6` | IPv6 | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |

<!-- prettier-ignore-end -->
